title: rosa dumalis var. scabrata
intro:
Rosa dumalis var. scabrata, also known as the scabrous rose, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the Rosa dumalis species, a fragrant and deciduous shrub native to Eastern Europe and Western Asia.

This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, primarily due to the presence of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and tannins in its leaves and stems, which have been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues. The scabrous rose is said to have numerous health benefits, including antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of colds, fever, and skin conditions. In herbal preparations, the leaves and stems of Rosa dumalis var. scabrata are often used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
